Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. She needs to calculate how many bottles of 14-ounce shampoo she needs for a leap year. A leap year has 366 days, so she will wash her hair 366 / 2 = 183 times. Each bottle provides 14 ounces of shampoo, and she uses 1/4 ounce per wash. To find the total amount of shampoo needed, we multiply 183 by 1/4, which equals 45.75 ounces. Since she cannot buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs to round up to the next whole number, which is 46 ounces. Each 14-ounce bottle provides 14 ounces, so she needs 46 / 14 = 3.2857 bottles. She must round up to 4 bottles to ensure she has enough shampoo for the entire leap year. [address] is the location where she buys the shampoo. [date] is the date when she starts using the shampoo. 

Brittany needs 4 bottles of shampoo for her leap year. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so 4 bottles provide 56 ounces of shampoo. She uses 1/4 ounce per wash, and she washes her hair 183 times. 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces. She needs 46 ounces of shampoo. 46 ounces divided by 14 ounces per bottle equals 3.2857 bottles. She must round up to 4 bottles to ensure she has enough shampoo. [address] is the location where she buys the shampoo. [date] is the date when she starts using the shampoo.
